   136→ *
   137→ * @return See lxl_sched_rc_e.
   138→ */
   139→int
   140→lxl_task_scheduler_start(lxl_task_scheduler_t *scheduler);
   141→
   142→/**
   143→ * lxl_task_scheduler_stop
   144→ *
   145→ * Gracefully stop or force-stop the scheduler.
   146→ *
   147→ * @param scheduler  Scheduler handle.
   148→ * @param force      true  → Immediately cancel all tasks and stop workers.
   149→ *                   false → Allow running tasks to complete first.
   150→ *
   151→ * @return See lxl_sched_rc_e.
   152→ */
   153→int
   154→lxl_task_scheduler_stop(lxl_task_scheduler_t *scheduler, bool force);
   155→
   156→/**
   157→ * lxl_task_scheduler_schedule
   158→ *
   159→ * Schedule a task that repeats at the specified interval.
   160→ *
   161→ * @param scheduler      Scheduler handle.
   162→ * @param task_id        Unique, NULL-terminated identifier (max LXL_TASK_ID_MAX-1).
   163→ * @param task_name      Human-readable name for dashboards/logs.
   164→ * @param cb             Task callback.
   165→ * @param user_ctx       User context passed verbatim to @cb.
   166→ * @param first_run      Epoch timestamp when the task should first execute.
   167→ *                       Pass 0 to run “as soon as possible”.
   168→ * @param interval_sec   Non-zero interval in seconds for recurring execution.
   169→ * @param priority       Higher numbers → executed before lower numbers when queued.
   170→ *
   171→ * @return See lxl_sched_rc_e.
   172→ */
   173→int
   174→lxl_task_scheduler_schedule(lxl_task_scheduler_t *scheduler,
   175→                            const char           *task_id,
   176→                            const char           *task_name,
   177→                            lxl_task_fn_t         cb,
   178→                            void                 *user_ctx,
   179→                            time_t                first_run,
   180→                            uint32_t              interval_sec,
   181→                            int                   priority);
   182→
   183→/**
   184→ * lxl_task_scheduler_schedule_once
   185→ *
   186→ * Schedule a one-shot task executed at the given timestamp.
   187→ *
   188→ * @param scheduler   Scheduler handle.
   189→ * @param task_id     Unique ID.
   190→ * @param task_name   Human-readable name.
   191→ * @param cb          Task callback.
   192→ * @param user_ctx    Opaque pointer handed back to @cb.
   193→ * @param run_at      Absolute epoch timestamp; 0 → run immediately.
   194→ * @param priority    Higher numbers → executed before lower numbers when queued.
   195→ *
   196→ * @return See lxl_sched_rc_e.
   197→ */
   198→int
   199→lxl_task_scheduler_schedule_once(lxl_task_scheduler_t *scheduler,
   200→                                 const char           *task_id,
   201→                                 const char           *task_name,
   202→                                 lxl_task_fn_t         cb,
   203→                                 void                 *user_ctx,
   204→                                 time_t                run_at,
   205→                                 int                   priority);
   206→
   207→/**
   208→ * lxl_task_scheduler_cancel
   209→ *
   210→ * Remove a task (pending or recurring) from the scheduler.
   211→ *
   212→ * @param scheduler  Scheduler handle.
   213→ * @param task_id    ID of the task to cancel.
   214→ *
   215→ * @return See lxl_sched_rc_e.
   216→ */
   217→int
   218→lxl_task_scheduler_cancel(lxl_task_scheduler_t *scheduler,
   219→                          const char           *task_id);
   220→
   221→/**
   222→ * lxl_task_scheduler_register_observer
   223→ *
   224→ * Register an observer for task-lifecycle events.
   225→ *
   226→ * @param scheduler  Scheduler handle.
   227→ * @param cb         Callback invoked on events (must be non-NULL).
   228→ * @param user_ctx   Pointer returned as-is in each event callback.
   229→ *
   230→ * @return See lxl_sched_rc_e.
   231→ *
   232→ * @warning The observer callback executes on the scheduler’s internal thread

   235→int

   238→                                     void                 *user_ctx);

   240→/**
   241→ * lxl_task_scheduler_get_pending_count

   245→ *
   246→ * @return Non-negative count on success, negative on failure (see lxl_sched_rc_e).
   247→ */
   248→int
   249→lxl_task_scheduler_get_pending_count(const lxl_task_scheduler_t *scheduler);
   250→
   251→/**
   252→ * lxl_task_scheduler_get_running_count
   253→ *
   254→ * Retrieve the number of tasks actively executing.
   255→ *
   256→ * @return Non-negative count on success, negative on failure.
   257→ */
   258→int
   259→lxl_task_scheduler_get_running_count(const lxl_task_scheduler_t *scheduler);
   260→
   261→#ifdef __cplusplus
   262→}   /* extern "C" */
   263→#endif
   264→
   265→#endif /* LEXILEARN_CONTROLLER_SCHEDULER_TASK_SCHEDULER_H */

1→/**
     2→ * File:    dashboard_server.h
     3→ * Author:  LexiLearn MVC Orchestrator – View Layer Team
     4→ * Brief:   Public interface for the DashboardServer – an HTTP/WebSocket server
     5→ *          responsible for streaming model-performance metrics, classroom
     6→ *          analytics, and explainability artifacts to educator dashboards.
     7→ *
     8→ * Copyright © 2023-2024 LexiLearn.
     9→ *
    10→ * NOTE:    The corresponding implementation is in dashboard_server.c.
    11→ *
    12→ * Thread-Safety:
    13→ * ------------
    14→ * DashboardServer is internally synchronized and can be safely accessed from
    15→ * multiple producer threads (e.g., model-monitor observers, retraining jobs)
    16→ * that publish real-time events to connected dashboard clients.
    17→ *
    18→ * Dependencies:
    19→ * -------------
    20→ *  • libwebsockets   (WebSocket I/O)
    21→ *  • cJSON           (JSON serialization)
    22→ *  • pthread         (concurrency primitives)
    23→ *  • openssl         (optional, TLS)
    24→ */
    25→
    26→#ifndef LEXILEARN_VIEW_DASHBOARD_SERVER_H
    27→#define LEXILEARN_VIEW_DASHBOARD_SERVER_H
    28→
    29→#ifdef __cplusplus
    30→extern "C" {
    31→#endif
    32→
    33→/*─────────────────────────── Standard Includes ─────────────────────────────*/
    34→#include <stddef.h>     /* size_t */
    35→#include <stdint.h>     /* uint16_t */
    36→#include <stdbool.h>    /* bool   */
    37→
    38→/*──────────────────────────── Project Includes ─────────────────────────────*/
    39→#include "../common/llog.h"  /* Project-wide logging macros */
    40→
    41→/*───────────────────────────── Versioning Info ─────────────────────────────*/
    42→#define DASHBOARD_SERVER_VERSION_MAJOR  1
    43→#define DASHBOARD_SERVER_VERSION_MINOR  0
    44→#define DASHBOARD_SERVER_VERSION_PATCH  0
    45→
    46→/*────────────────────────────── Error Codes ────────────────────────────────*/
    47→typedef enum
    48→{
    49→    DB_ERR_OK             = 0,   /* Success                                     */
    50→    DB_ERR_INIT           = 1,   /* Initialization failure                      */
    51→    DB_ERR_BIND           = 2,   /* Port binding error                          */
    52→    DB_ERR_TLS            = 3,   /* TLS/SSL initialization error                */
    53→    DB_ERR_WEBSOCKET      = 4,   /* WebSocket related failure                   */
    54→    DB_ERR_INVALID_ARG    = 5,   /* Bad user input                              */
    55→    DB_ERR_ROUTE_EXISTS   = 6,   /* Handler already registered for route/method */
    56→    DB_ERR_NO_MEM         = 7,   /* Out of memory                               */
    57→    DB_ERR_INTERNAL       = 8    /* Unspecified internal error                  */
    58→} db_err_t;
    59→
    60→/*───────────────────────────── Forward Decls ───────────────────────────────*/
    61→struct DashboardServer;
    62→
    63→/* Opaque server handle. */
    64→typedef struct DashboardServer DashboardServer;
    65→
    66→/*───────────────────────────── Public Typedefs ─────────────────────────────*/
    67→/**
    68→ * Callback prototype for custom HTTP route handlers registered by the
    69→ * Controller layer to expose REST endpoints (e.g., /api/retrain).
    70→ *
    71→ * Parameters
    72→ * ----------
    73→ *  uri            – full request URI (null-terminated)
    74→ *  request_body   – raw request body (may be NULL), not guaranteed to be '\0'
    75→ *  request_len    – byte length of request_body
    76→ *  response_body  – [out] Implementation must allocate and set. The caller
    77→ *                   (DashboardServer) will free via free().
    78→ *  response_len   – [out] Length of *response_body in bytes.
    79→ *  user_ctx       – arbitrary pointer supplied on registration.
    80→ *
    81→ * Returns
    82→ * -------
    83→ *  HTTP status code such as 200, 400, 500, etc.
    84→ */
    85→typedef int (*dashboard_route_cb)(
    86→        const char  *uri,
    87→        const char  *request_body,
    88→        size_t       request_len,
    89→        char       **response_body,
    90→        size_t      *response_len,
    91→        void        *user_ctx);
    92→
    93→/*──────────────────────────── Server Options ───────────────────────────────*/
    94→/**
    95→ * DashboardServerOptions – immutable configuration passed at creation time.
    96→ */
    97→typedef struct
    98→{
    99→    uint16_t  port;                 /* TCP port to listen on                   */
   100→    bool      enable_tls;           /* true = HTTPS/WSS, false = HTTP/WS       */
   101→    const char *tls_cert_path;      /* PEM-encoded cert chain (required if TLS)*/
   102→    const char *tls_key_path;       /* PEM-encoded private key (required if TLS)*/
   103→    const char *static_asset_dir;   /* Document root for HTML/JS assets        */
   104→    unsigned   max_clients;         /* Hard limit on concurrent clients (0=∞)  */
   105→    bool       enable_cors;         /* If true, adds Access-Control-Allow-*    */
   106→} DashboardServerOptions;
   107→
   108→/*────────────────────────────── API Surface ───────────────────────────────*/
   109→/**
   110→ * Create a new DashboardServer instance.
   111→ *
   112→ * The server is not listening after creation; call dashboard_server_start().
   113→ *
   114→ * Returns
   115→ * -------
   116→ *  • Pointer to a new DashboardServer on success.
   117→ *  • NULL on failure (inspect errno or llog error output).
   118→ */
   119→DashboardServer *dashboard_server_create(const DashboardServerOptions *opts);
   120→
   121→/**
   122→ * Start the I/O threads and begin accepting client connections.
   123→ *
   124→ * This call is non-blocking; it spawns internal worker threads and returns.
   125→ */
   126→db_err_t dashboard_server_start(DashboardServer *srv);
   127→
   128→/**
   129→ * Register a custom HTTP handler.
   130→ *
   131→ * Example:
   132→ *      dashboard_server_register_route(srv, "POST", "/api/retrain",
   133→ *                                      retrain_handler, my_ctx);
   134→ *
   135→ * Limitations:
   136→ *  • Route string must start with '/'.
   137→ *  • Method is case-insensitive and must be one of GET, POST, PUT, DELETE.
   138→ */
   139→db_err_t dashboard_server_register_route(
   140→        DashboardServer    *srv,
   141→        const char         *method,
   142→        const char         *route,
   143→        dashboard_route_cb  cb,
   144→        void               *user_ctx);
   145→
   146→/**
   147→ * Publish a JSON message to a named channel. All WebSocket clients that have
   148→ * subscribed to this channel will receive the message asynchronously.
   149→ *
   150→ * Internally, the function copies `json_payload`; the caller retains ownership.
   151→ */
   152→db_err_t dashboard_server_publish(
   153→        DashboardServer    *srv,
   154→        const char         *channel_name,
   155→        const char         *json_payload);
   156→
   157→/**
   158→ * Broadcast a binary blob to every connected WebSocket client, regardless of
   159→ * subscription filtering. Intended for large artifacts like model heat-maps.
   160→ */
   161→db_err_t dashboard_server_broadcast(
   162→        DashboardServer    *srv,
   163→        const void         *data,
   164→        size_t              data_len,
   165→        const char         *mime_type /* e.g., "image/png" */);
   166→
   167→/**
   168→ * Gracefully shut down the server: closes all client connections, flushes
   169→ * outbound queues, and joins background threads.
   170→ */
   171→void dashboard_server_stop(DashboardServer *srv);
   172→
   173→/**
   174→ * Destroy the server instance and reclaim resources. Safe to call on a NULL
   175→ * pointer. The server MUST be stopped before calling this function.
   176→ */
   177→void dashboard_server_destroy(DashboardServer *srv);
   178→
   179→/*────────────────────────── Convenience Utilities ──────────────────────────*/
   180→/**
   181→ * Helper that blocks the caller (usually the main thread) until a termination
   182→ * signal (SIGINT/SIGTERM) is received, at which point it triggers a graceful
   183→ * shutdown on the supplied DashboardServer.
   184→ *
   185→ * Returns DB_ERR_OK when the server terminates cleanly.
   186→ */
   187→db_err_t dashboard_server_block_until_signal(DashboardServer *srv);
   188→
   189→/**
   190→ * Retrieve the semantic version string (e.g., "1.0.0").
   191→ * The returned pointer is to a static buffer—do NOT free().
   192→ */
   193→const char *dashboard_server_version(void);
   194→
   195→#ifdef __cplusplus
   196→} /* extern "C" */
   197→#endif
   198→
   199→#endif /* LEXILEARN_VIEW_DASHBOARD_SERVER_H */
   200→

1→/**
     2→ *  LexiLearn MVC Orchestrator – Model Registry Public Interface
     3→ *  -------------------------------------------------------------
     4→ *  File:    model_registry.h
     5→ *  Project: LexiLearn NLP / ML Pipeline
     6→ *
     7→ *  Author:  LexiLearn Core Team
     8→ *  License: Apache 2.0
     9→ *
    10→ *  Description:
    11→ *      Production-grade, thread-safe C API for LexiLearn’s on-disk /
    12→ *      remote (future) Model Registry.  The registry tracks the full
    13→ *      life-cycle of ML models—versioning, stage promotion, metadata,
    14→ *      and lineage—for reproducible research and robust MLOps.
    15→ *
    16→ *  Usage:
    17→ *      #include "model_registry.h"
    18→ *
    19→ *      if (ml_registry_init("/var/lib/lexilearn/registry") != ML_OK)
    20→ *          die(ml_registry_last_error());
    21→ *
    22→ *      char model_id[ML_MODEL_ID_MAX] = {0};
    23→ *      ml_registry_register_model("hybrid-summarizer",
    24→ *                                 "v1.3.4",
    25→ *                                 "/models/hybrid-summarizer_v1.3.4.bin",
    26→ *                                 "{\"lr\":0.0001, \"epochs\":12}",
    27→ *                                 "{\"val_bleu\":0.87}",
    28→ *                                 "b01cbe6",
    29→ *                                 model_id,
    30→ *                                 sizeof(model_id));
    31→ *
    32→ *      ml_registry_shutdown();
    33→ */
    34→
    35→#ifndef LEXILEARN_MODEL_REGISTRY_H
    36→#define LEXILEARN_MODEL_REGISTRY_H
    37→
    38→/* ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    39→/*  System Headers                                                           */
    40→/* ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    41→#include <stddef.h>     /* size_t      */
    42→#include <stdint.h>     /* uint64_t    */
    43→#include <time.h>       /* time_t      */
    44→#include <stdio.h>      /* FILE        */
    45→
    46→#ifdef __cplusplus
    47→extern "C" {
    48→#endif
    49→
    50→/* ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    51→/*  Feature Flags / Config                                                   */
    52→/* ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    53→
    54→#ifndef ML_REGISTRY_THREAD_SAFE
    55→#define ML_REGISTRY_THREAD_SAFE 1
    56→#endif
    57→
    58→/* Maximum byte length for a model identifier (“{strategy}:{hash}”)          */
    59→#define ML_MODEL_ID_MAX          128
    60→
    61→/* JSON blobs are stored in TEXT columns – reasonable upper bound            */
    62→#define ML_JSON_MAX              8192
    63→
    64→/* Strategy name ≤ 64 chars; version tag ≤ 32 chars                          */
    65→#define ML_STRATEGY_MAX          64
    66→#define ML_VERSION_TAG_MAX       32
    67→
    68→/* Internal filesystem limits                                               */
    69→#define ML_PATH_MAX              4096
    70→
    71→/* ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    72→/*  Error Handling                                                           */
    73→/* ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    74→
    75→typedef enum
    76→{
    77→    ML_OK = 0,
    78→    ML_ERR_INIT          = -1,
    79→    ML_ERR_SHUTDOWN      = -2,
    80→    ML_ERR_IO            = -3,
    81→    ML_ERR_MEMORY        = -4,
    82→    ML_ERR_INVALID_ARG   = -5,
    83→    ML_ERR_NOT_FOUND     = -6,
    84→    ML_ERR_CONFLICT      = -7,
    85→    ML_ERR_SERIALIZATION = -8,
    86→    ML_ERR_UNKNOWN       = -100
    87→} ml_error_t;
    88→
    89→/**
    90→ * Returns a thread-local, NULL-terminated string describing the last error.
    91→ * The pointer is owned by the registry and MUST NOT be freed by the caller.
    92→ */
    93→const char *ml_registry_last_error(void);
    94→
    95→/* ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    96→/*  Model Metadata Structures                                                */
    97→/* ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    98→
    99→/* Model stage / life-cycle gates                                            */
   100→typedef enum
   101→{
   102→    ML_STAGE_EXPERIMENT = 0,  /* Default after registration                 */
   103→    ML_STAGE_STAGING    = 1,  /* Candidate awaiting production sign-off     */
   104→    ML_STAGE_PRODUCTION = 2,  /* Actively serving recommendations           */
   105→    ML_STAGE_ARCHIVED   = 3   /* Frozen for audit or rollback               */
   106→} ml_model_stage_t;
   107→
   108→/* Primary key is a deterministic string: <strategy>::<git-sha>              */
   109→typedef struct
   110→{
   111→    char             model_id[ML_MODEL_ID_MAX];   /* PK                     */
   112→    char             strategy[ML_STRATEGY_MAX];   /* Strategy pattern key   */
   113→    char             version_tag[ML_VERSION_TAG_MAX]; /* “v1.2.3”           */
   114→    char             artifact_path[ML_PATH_MAX];  /* Path to binary / pkl   */
   115→
   116→    /* JSON blobs: hyper-parameters, metrics, & additional metadata          */
   117→    char             hyperparams_json[ML_JSON_MAX];
   118→    char             metrics_json[ML_JSON_MAX];
   119→
   120→    char             git_commit_sha[16];          /* short SHA (7–15 chars) */
   121→    ml_model_stage_t stage;                       /* Current stage          */
   122→
   123→    time_t           created_at;                  /* Registration timestamp */
   124→    time_t           last_updated;                /* Stage promotion, etc.  */
   125→
   126→} ml_model_record_t;
   127→
   128→/* ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
   129→/*  Registry Lifecycle                                                       */
   130→/* ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
   131→
   132→/**
   133→ * Initializes / opens the registry at the given root directory.
   134→ * This call is idempotent.  Multiple opens simply bump an internal ref-count.
   135→ *
   136→ * Parameters:
   137→ *   registry_root – absolute path to directory containing registry.db & blobs
   138→ *
   139→ * Returns:
   140→ *   ML_OK on success, negative error code otherwise.
   141→ */
   142→ml_error_t ml_registry_init(const char *registry_root);
   143→
   144→/**
   145→ * Flushes pending IO and releases resources.  Balanced with ml_registry_init().
   146→ * The registry may remain open if other references are active.
   147→ */
   148→ml_error_t ml_registry_shutdown(void);
   149→
   150→/* ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
   151→/*  CRUD – Create                                                            */
   152→/* ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
   153→
   154→/**
   155→ * Registers a new model artifact & metadata into the registry.  Function is
   156→ * atomic; either all metadata are flushed to disk or an error is returned.
   157→ *
   158→ * Parameters:
   159→ *   strategy_name     – logical family (e.g., “transformer-summarizer”)
   160→ *   version_tag       – human-readable tag (“v2.1-alpha”)
   161→ *   artifact_path     – absolute / relative path to binary artifact
   162→ *   hyperparams_json  – serialized hyperparameters (may be NULL / "")
   163→ *   metrics_json      – serialized evaluation metrics (may be NULL / "")
   164→ *   git_commit_sha    – optional SCM commit for code–model lineage
   165→ *   out_model_id      – buffer where unique model_id will be written
   166→ *   out_model_id_len  – size of the above buffer
   167→ *
   168→ * Returns:
   169→ *   ML_OK on success, error code otherwise.
   170→ */
   171→ml_error_t ml_registry_register_model(const char *strategy_name,
   172→                                      const char *version_tag,
   173→                                      const char *artifact_path,
   174→                                      const char *hyperparams_json,

     2→ *  LexiLearn Orchestrator: Model Strategy Interface
     3→ *  File: lexilearn_orchestrator/src/model/ml_models/model_strategy.h
     4→ *
     5→ *  Description:
     6→ *      This header formalises the Strategy Pattern contract for the Model layer.
     7→ *      Concrete strategies (e.g. transformer_summarizer.c, ngram_tagger.c, or
     8→ *      hybrid_meta_learner.c) must expose a constructor that returns an
     9→ *      instance whose vptr satisfies the model_strategy_vtable_t defined here.
    10→ *
    11→ *      At runtime, the Controller layer requests a strategy by name.  A small
    12→ *      registry implemented in model_strategy.c maps names to constructors,
    13→ *      allowing educators and researchers to swap algorithms without touching
    14→ *      Controller code or recompiling the full orchestrator.
    15→ *
    16→ *  Notes:
    17→ *      • All functions return lexi_status_t for uniform error handling.
    18→ *      • The interface is intentionally agnostic to the underlying ML
    19→ *        framework (ONNX, TorchScript, custom C kernels, etc.).
    20→ *      • This file is pure C and therefore includes an extern "C" guard so
    21→ *        that strategies may be implemented in C++ when convenient.
    22→ *
    23→ *  Author:  LexiLearn Engineering
    24→ *  License: Apache-2.0
    25→ */
    26→
    27→#ifndef LEXILEARN_MODEL_STRATEGY_H
    28→#define LEXILEARN_MODEL_STRATEGY_H
    29→
    30→#include <stddef.h>   /* size_t */
    31→#include <stdint.h>   /* int64_t */
    32→#include <stdbool.h>  /* bool */
    33→
    34→#ifdef __cplusplus
    35→extern "C" {
    36→#endif
    37→
    38→/*---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    39→ * Global status / error-code enumeration
    40→ *--------------------------------------------------------------------------*/
    41→typedef enum
    42→{
    43→    LEXI_OK                    =  0,
    44→    LEXI_ERR_INVALID_ARGUMENT  = -1,
    45→    LEXI_ERR_NO_MEMORY         = -2,
    46→    LEXI_ERR_IO                = -3,
    47→    LEXI_ERR_NOT_IMPLEMENTED   = -4,
    48→    LEXI_ERR_INTERNAL          = -5
    49→} lexi_status_t;
    50→
    51→/*---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    52→ * Task types supported by LexiLearn.  Used for routing to the right model.
    53→ *--------------------------------------------------------------------------*/
    54→typedef enum
    55→{
    56→    TASK_SUMMARIZATION = 0,
    57→    TASK_GRAMMAR_CORRECTION,
    58→    TASK_PROFICIENCY_SCORING,
    59→    TASK_KEYWORD_EXTRACTION,
    60→    TASK_CUSTOM
    61→} lexi_task_t;
    62→
    63→/*---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    64→ * Generic hyper-parameter container
    65→ *--------------------------------------------------------------------------*/
    66→typedef enum
    67→{
    68→    HP_TYPE_INT,
    69→    HP_TYPE_DOUBLE,
    70→    HP_TYPE_BOOL,
    71→    HP_TYPE_STRING
    72→} hp_type_t;
    73→
    74→typedef struct
    75→{
    76→    const char *key;    /* e.g. "learning_rate" */
    77→    hp_type_t   type;
    78→    union
    79→    {
    80→        int64_t     i;
    81→        double      d;
    82→        bool        b;
    83→        const char *s;
    84→    } value;
    85→} hyperparam_t;
    86→
    87→/*---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    88→ * Forward declarations of opaque resource types used across the system
    89→ *--------------------------------------------------------------------------*/
    90→typedef struct ll_dataset_t        ll_dataset_t;        /* defined in dataset.h */
    91→typedef struct ll_feature_store_t  ll_feature_store_t;  /* defined in feature_store.h */
    92→typedef struct ll_model_artifact_t ll_model_artifact_t; /* defined in registry.h */
    93→
    94→/*---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    95→ * Strategy object & vtable
    96→ *--------------------------------------------------------------------------*/
    97→struct model_strategy_vtable; /* fwd */
    98→
    99→typedef struct model_strategy
   100→{
   101→    const char *name;                           /* strategy identifier */
   102→    const struct model_strategy_vtable *vptr;   /* method table        */
   103→    void *impl;                                 /* strategy-specific data */
   104→} model_strategy_t;
   105→
   106→/*
   107→ *  Virtual table defining the lifecycle of a model strategy.
   108→ *
   109→ *  Implementations MUST fill in all mandatory function pointers.  Optional
   110→ *  operations (e.g. load/save) may return LEXI_ERR_NOT_IMPLEMENTED when
   111→ *  unsupported.
   112→ */
   113→typedef struct model_strategy_vtable
   114→{
   115→    /*
   116→     *  configure():
   117→     *      Apply a list of hyper-parameters prior to training.
   118→     *
   119→     *  Return:
   120→     *      LEXI_OK on success, or appropriate error code.
   121→     */
   122→    lexi_status_t (*configure)(model_strategy_t      *self,
   123→                               const hyperparam_t    *params,
   124→                               size_t                 param_count);
   125→
   126→    /*
   127→     *  train():
   128→     *      Fit the model to the given datasets.  The model artefact emitted
   129→     *      here is registered with the Model Registry by the Controller.
   130→     */
   131→    lexi_status_t (*train)(model_strategy_t      *self,
   132→                           const ll_dataset_t    *training_data,
   133→                           const ll_dataset_t    *validation_data,
   134→                           ll_model_artifact_t  **out_model);
   135→
   136→    /*
   137→     *  predict():
   138→     *      Run inference using the current state of the model.
   139→     *      Predictions are returned as a dataset so that downstream pipeline
   140→     *      stages can treat them uniformly.
   141→     */
   142→    lexi_status_t (*predict)(model_strategy_t   *self,
   143→                             const ll_dataset_t *input_data,
   144→                             ll_dataset_t      **out_predictions);
   145→
   146→    /*
   147→     *  evaluate():
   148→     *      Compute an evaluation metric (e.g. BLEU, ROUGE-L, accuracy).
   149→     *      Metric choice is strategy-specific.
   150→     */
   151→    lexi_status_t (*evaluate)(model_strategy_t   *self,
   152→                              const ll_dataset_t *test_data,
   153→                              double             *out_score);
   154→
   155→    /*
   156→     *  save() / load():
   157→     *      Persist or restore the model.  Implementation may stream to/from
   158→     *      cloud object storage, local disk, or a database URI.
   159→     */
   160→    lexi_status_t (*save)(model_strategy_t *self,
   161→                          const char       *artifact_uri);
   162→
   163→    lexi_status_t (*load)(model_strategy_t *self,
   164→                          const char       *artifact_uri);
   165→
   166→    /*
   167→     *  destroy():
   168→     *      Finaliser invoked by the orchestrator when the strategy is no
   169→     *      longer needed.  Must free all memory and release GPU/CPU handles.
   170→     */
   171→    void (*destroy)(model_strategy_t *self);
   172→
   173→} model_strategy_vtable_t;
   174→
   175→/*---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   176→ * Registry API
   177→ *  – Facilitates dynamic discovery and instantiation of strategies
   178→ *---------------------------------------------------------------------------*/
   179→
   180→/* Prototype that concrete strategies must expose */
   181→typedef model_strategy_t *(*strategy_ctor_fn)(void);
   182→
   183→/*
   184→ *  ll_strategy_register():
   185→ *      Register a constructor under a unique, case-insensitive name.
   186→ *
   187→ *  Typical usage (inside transformer_summarizer.c):
   188→ *      static model_strategy_t *transformer_ctor(void) { … }
   189→ *      __attribute__((constructor))
   190→ *      static void _init(void)
   191→ *      {
   192→ *          ll_strategy_register("transformer_summarizer", transformer_ctor);
   193→ *      }
   194→ */
   195→lexi_status_t ll_strategy_register(const char      *name,
   196→                                   strategy_ctor_fn ctor);
   197→
   198→/*
   199→ *  ll_strategy_create():
   200→ *      Instantiate a strategy by name.  Caller owns the returned pointer and
   201→ *      must eventually invoke ->vptr->destroy().
   202→ */
   203→model_strategy_t *ll_strategy_create(const char *name);
   204→
   205→/*
   206→ *  ll_strategy_list():
   207→ *      Enumerate all names currently registered.  The returned array is
   208→ *      heap-allocated and must be freed via ll_strategy_free_name_list().
   209→ */
   210→void ll_strategy_list(char ***out_names, size_t *out_count);
   211→
   212→/* Release memory allocated by ll_strategy_list(). */
   213→void ll_strategy_free_name_list(char **names, size_t count);
   214→
   215→#ifdef __cplusplus
   216→} /* extern "C" */
   217→#endif
   218→
   219→#endif /* LEXILEARN_MODEL_STRATEGY_H */

The model artefact emitted\n     *      here is registered with the Model Registry by the Controller.\n     */\n    lexi_status_t (*train)(model_strategy_t      *self,\n                           const ll_dataset_t    *training_data,\n                           const ll_dataset_t    *validation_data,\n                           ll_model_artifact_t  **out_model);\n\n    /*\n     *  predict():\n     *      Run inference using the current state of the model.\n     *      Predictions are returned as a dataset so that downstream pipeline\n     *      stages can treat them uniformly.\n     */\n    lexi_status_t (*predict)(model_strategy_t   *self,\n                             const ll_dataset_t *input_data,\n                             ll_dataset_t      **out_predictions);\n\n    /*\n     *  evaluate():\n     *      Compute an evaluation metric (e.g. BLEU, ROUGE-L, accuracy).\n     *      Metric choice is strategy-specific.\n     */\n    lexi_status_t (*evaluate)(model_strategy_t   *self,\n                              const ll_dataset_t *test_data,\n                              double             *out_score);\n\n    /*\n     *  save() / load():\n     *      Persist or restore the model.  Implementation may stream to/from\n     *      cloud object storage, local disk, or a database URI.\n     */\n    lexi_status_t (*save)(model_strategy_t *self,\n                          const char       *artifact_uri);\n\n    lexi_status_t (*load)(model_strategy_t *self,\n                          const char       *artifact_uri);\n\n    /*\n     *  destroy():\n     *      Finaliser invoked by the orchestrator when the strategy is no\n     *      longer needed.  Must free all memory and release GPU/CPU handles.\n     */\n    void (*destroy)(model_strategy_t *self);\n\n} model_strategy_vtable_t;\n\n/*---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Registry API\n *  – Facilitates dynamic discovery and instantiation of strategies\n *---------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n/* Prototype that concrete strategies must expose */\ntypedef model_strategy_t *(*strategy_ctor_fn)(void);\n\n/*\n *  ll_strategy_register():\n *      Register a constructor under a unique, case-insensitive name.\n *\n *  Typical usage (inside transformer_summarizer.c):\n *      static model_strategy_t *transformer_ctor(void) { … }\n *      __attribute__((constructor))\n *      static void _init(void)\n *      {\n *          ll_strategy_register(\"transformer_summarizer\", transformer_ctor);\n *      }\n */\nlexi_status_t ll_strategy_register(const char      *name,\n                                   strategy_ctor_fn ctor);\n\n/*\n *  ll_strategy_create():\n *      Instantiate a strategy by name.  Caller owns the returned pointer and\n *      must eventually invoke ->vptr->destroy().\n */\nmodel_strategy_t *ll_strategy_create(const char *name);\n\n/*\n *  ll_strategy_list():\n *      Enumerate all names currently registered.  The returned array is\n *      heap-allocated and must be freed via ll_strategy_free_name_list().\n */\nvoid ll_strategy_list(char ***out_names, size_t *out_count);\n\n/* Release memory allocated by ll_strategy_list(). */\nvoid ll_strategy_free_name_list(char **names, size_t count);\n\n#ifdef __cplusplus\n} /* extern \"C\" */\n#endif\n\n#endif /* LEXILEARN_MODEL_STRATEGY_H */\n", "numLines": 220, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 220}}
